mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising industrial production, positive supply-side momentum supports corporate output and inventory turnover, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns do not materialize in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO rises during recovery phases, or the mechanism reverses direction during inflationary supply constraints.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures reflect strong household spending power, which directly boosts near-term corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no directional bias when RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy curtails credit availability and increases discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market downturns, sharp declines in housing starts signal wealth destruction and reduced construction activity, which immediately triggers risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not occur in more than 65% of triggered instances during housing downturns, or the signal loses predictive power when HOUST declines are accompanied by aggressive Fed rate cuts.
